5G WIRELESS INTERNET BACKUP SALES BATTLE CARD

KEY SELLING POINTS

QUICK PITCH

PAIN POINTS OUR SOLUTION PROVIDES...

In todayÔÇÖs business environment,  reliable connectivity is critical. Power  outages or unforeseen fiber cuts  can bring your operations to a halt,  impacting productivity and revenue.

Backed by Experts:  The service is backed by  highly rated brands such as Verizon, AT& T and  Cradlepoint

A continuous connection, allowing your  business to continue running as normal  even during fiber cuts or outages

Small businesses suffer when the Internet  goes down and they cannot provide goods or  services to customers.

Affordable Back-Up:  High-quality backup  solution at a more affordable price than a  secondary wired connection

Prioritization of 5G bandwidth to your most  critical applications, whether thatÔÇÖs your POS  system or customer Wi-Fi

Patrons using public WiFi in coffee shops or  restaurants will take their business elsewhere  if there is not a reliable Internet connection.

ThatÔÇÖs why Great Plains  Communications offers 5G  Internet Backup to keep your  business running.  It automatically  switches your network to a secure,  5G connection when your primary  Internet goes down while prioritizing  the wireless bandwidth to your most  critical applications and systems.  Contact us today to ensure your  business stays seamlessly connected!

Continued Sales:  Automatic failover  connectivity means no lost sales

Automatic failover to ensure you never lose  a sale by keeping your POS and credit card  terminals online during outages

An inconsistent Internet connection can affect  the ability to process credit card payments  and sales could suffer as a result.

Expert Monitoring:  Monitored and managed  in GPCÔÇÖs 24/7 Network Operations Center

Companies that rely on Cloud services for  their day-to-day operations will be unable to  operate if the Internet is down.

An automatic failover to 5G, keeping your  connection to the Cloud online.

Best Signal Available:  Automatically selects  the 5G provider with the best signal strength  and quality at your location

A fast, affordable back-up Internet solution  to keep your business connected during  outages

Redundant wired access is too expensive  and complex for most small to medium-sized  businesses.

Connected During Power Outages:  Optional  back-up battery keeps your business running  for up to 8 hours even during power outages

QUALIFYING QUESTIONS CHALLENGE

TARGET COMPANY  CHARACTERISTICS

GPC is competing against national carriers like Cox,  Lumen, Spectrum or Windstream, in addition to  direct competition from the providers providing the  back-up service, Verizon and AT&T, that also offer a  Wireless Internet Backup service.

How many people need Internet at this location? 
 (Recommended: 25 or less)

┬╗ Small healthcare clinics who need continuous  access to online patient records

┬╗ Retailers and restaurants who use POS  terminals and inventory management systems

Have you had Internet outages in the past?

┬╗ Companies whose applications are moving to  or are in the Cloud

What applications would be impacted if your Internet went down?

RESPONSE

Do you have Point-of-Sale or credit card terminals that rely on the  Internet?

┬╗ Local government offices required to have a  redundant, backup Internet connection

GPCÔÇÖs 5G Wireless Internet Backup is backed by the  best technology available combined with the local  access and support you wonÔÇÖt receive from national  providers.

┬╗ Top industries: Healthcare, Retail, Financial  Services, Automotive and Municipal  Governments

Do you host servers on-site that are accessed over the Internet?  Those servers will not be accessible during failover to 5G.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

GPC  MANAGED ETHERNET

CUSTOMER  PAIN POINTS

TARGET  AUDIENCE

Network Manager

CIO

Insufficient bandwidth over leased  lines or highly asymmetrical  broadband access can reduce the  quality of the end-user experience.

Data Center Manager

IT Manager

E-Rate Consultant (Education)

Network Engineer

Telecom Manager

Traffic over the public Internet  is subject to variable amounts of  packet loss, delay and jitter that  can disrupt real-time voice or video  communications.

INDUSTRIES

Healthcare High bandwidth and consistent  quality to support telemedicine,  medical imaging and cloud- based applications

The public Internet is insecure  and not well suited for connecting  branch offices, central hubs and  data centers without adding costly  layers of security.

Financial Services Low-latency and diversely  routed circuits

Government Multiple sites and local data  storage requirements suited  for point-to-multipoint  configurations

Low-bandwidth MPLS services  have limited throughput that can  impact the performance of latency- sensitive applications.

Education Campus and remote sites can  benefit from high performance,  value and flexibility

Lack of redundant and diverse  access leaves critical sites  vulnerable to unpredictable  outages caused by fiber cuts.

KEY  FEATURES

Private Fiber Optic Network:  Ethernet over fiber provides inherent security with virtually  unlimited bandwidth and offers superior quality of service.

Diverse Access:   Diverse access options (available for an extra charge) and diverse routes provide  business continuity and disaster recovery for critical facilities such as hub sites and data centers.

Multiple Configurations:  Help control the cost of connecting multiple sites with point-to-point,  point-to-multipoint or multipoint-to-multipoint configurations.

Ethernet Service Level Agreement (SLA):  The Ethernet SLA guarantees up to 99.999% availability  as well as key performance metrics such as packet loss, delay and jitter.

MEF 3.0 Certified:  GPCÔÇÖs Ethernet services have been certified through a rigorous evaluation  process by the industry consortium for Carrier Ethernet.

Businesses need fast, stable, and always-on

Internet. Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite such as

Quick Sales  Pitch

Starlink offers high-speed internet service, but its

performance depends on weather, sky visibility

and shared constellations. Latency swings and

environmental factors can impact real-time

applications, and the hardware is expensive for a

small business budget.

Great Plains Communications delivers true

fiber-to-the-premises connectivity, offering

unmatched reliability, symmetrical speeds,

and local support all at an affordable price,

benefits satellite technology simply cannot

match.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

GPC  CLOUD CONNECT

TARGET  CUSTOMERS

Industries Include:

ÔÇó	 Healthcare

ÔÇó	 Government - State  and Municipal

Mid-sized to large enterprises and organizations  with principal facilities within Great Plains  Communications (GPC) territory

ÔÇó	 Technology

ÔÇó	 Education - School  Systems, Colleges  and Universities

ÔÇó	 Manufacturing

ÔÇó	 Financial Services -  Banking, Insurance  and Investment

Current and prospective GPC Ethernet  customers with 50 Mbps or higher who require  access to applications in the cloud

CUSTOMER  PAIN POINTS

Corporate workloads have migrated from  private data centers to public clouds  outside the corporate security perimeter.

Accessing applications over the public  Internet provides inconsistent security 
 and performance.

There is high cost and complexity  associated to connecting to multiple  network and Cloud Service Providers.

It is difficult to manage and  troubleshoot applications that are  accessed via multiple providers.

It is difficult to match changing  workload and application demands  due to lack of bandwidth scalability.

KEY  FEATURES

GPC Fiber Ethernet:  High bandwidth, Layer 2 transport network with a rigorous service level  agreement (SLA) for high availability and performance

Multi-Cloud Connectivity:  Provides direct connections with leading Cloud Service Providers

┬╗ Standard Access  for AWS, Azure, Google and IBM

┬╗ ICB  for Salesforce, SAP, Nutanix and Alibaba

Partnership with Leading Providers:   Partner with leading providers of cloud access and  consulting for architecture and design

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

GPC  SD-WAN

TARGET  CUSTOMERS

Industries Include:

ÔÇó	 Healthcare

ÔÇó	 Government - State  and Municipal

Enterprises and organizations with at least  one network connection to the Great Plains  Communications (GPC) fiber network.

ÔÇó	 Financial Services -  Banking, Insurance  and Investment

ÔÇó	 Education - School  Systems, Colleges  and Universities

ÔÇó	 Insurance

Multi-site customers within the GPC footprint  with off-net offices located anywhere in the  continental United States.

ÔÇó	 Real Estate  Development

ÔÇó	 Retail

ÔÇó	 Construction

CUSTOMER  PAIN POINTS

Accessing cloud-based applications over  the public Internet can reduce the quality  of experience for end-users.

Backhauling all traffic to a centralized  firewall is inefficient and decreases  application responsiveness.

Expensive, low-bandwidth MPLS services  and lack of redundant access leaves  networks vulnerable to outages.

Traditional edge routers are not  centrally managed and must be  manually configured one by one.

The growth of real-time voice and video  communications over the Internet has led to  inconsistent performance and costly upgrades.

KEY  FEATURES

Centralized Monitoring and Control:  Orchestrator centralizes configuration, monitoring and  troubleshooting for all network edge devices

Simplified WAN Management:  Point-and-click GUI visibility from the access links to the  applications on a single pane of glass

Dynamic Multi-Path Optimization Ôäó :  Application-aware packet steering onto the best path  with sub-second failover to improve application response time and uptime

Dynamic Link Performance Enhancement:  Improve performance over underlying access  links by correcting for packet loss, delay and jitter

Maximize Application Performance:  Business policy prioritization optimizes the performance  of critical applications

MANAGED FIREWALL  powered by Fortinet ┬«

SALES BATTLE CARD

KEY SELLING POINTS

QUICK PITCH

PAIN POINTS OUR SOLUTION PROVIDES...

Cost-Effective:  No up-front  equipment purchase and reduced IT  overhead

As the amount of cyberattacks  increase, the need to align  network and security has become  increasingly important. In addition,  most organizations lack the  cybersecurity skills to ensure  networks are protected from  cyber criminals. GPCÔÇÖs Managed  Firewall paired with managed  security will free your IT team to  focus on strategic initiatives while  providing the peace of mind that  comes from knowing your networks  are protected by best-in-class  technology and are supported  by experienced, expert technical  security support. Partner with GPC  today and rely on an experienced  single provider for your Internet,  networking and security needs.

Continuous monitoring of network traffic for  potential threats and blocking of malicious  traffic before it hits your network

Lack of adequate security puts the smaller  business and its customers at high risk

All-in-One Solution:  SD-WAN and  security over GPCÔÇÖs fiber Internet

Access to experienced security experts  to configure, monitor and manage your  network firewalls

Lack of IT staff with training, experience and  certifications to adequately configure and  support network firewalls

Proactive Performance:   24x7x365 proactive monitoring  and management from a Security  Operations Center (SOC)

Multiple providers for networking and security  leads to finger-pointing and confusion when  incidents arise

A single provider and point of contact for  Internet, SD-WAN and security services

Expert Design:  Professional firewall  configuration for comprehensive  protection and optimal performance

Network security and SD-WAN management  that allows internal IT teams to focus on the  core business

Complexity of managing distributed firewalls  at every branch office and slow performance  of backhauling traffic to a centralized firewall

Enterprise Protection:  Fortinet  FortiGuard Enterprise Protection  offers the most comprehensive set  of security features available

Regular software and firmware updates and  patch management on firewalls to remain  effective

Firewall management that includes software  updates and patch management

Service Consolidation:  One  provider and single point of contact  for Internet, SD-WAN, voice and  network security

24x7x365 proactive monitoring from a SOC  II certified Security Operations Center (SOC)  staffed by security engineers

Lack of 24/7 support staff in the event of a  cyber attack

QUALIFYING QUESTIONS CHALLENGE

TARGET COMPANY  CHARACTERISTICS

COLLATERAL

Most potential customers already have  Internet with firewalls and other security  services from someone - another carrier or  MSP, independent IT vendor or themselves.  They may not be interested in adding yet  another vendor.

┬╗ Multiple sites

┬╗ Companies that transport  sensitive customer data

┬╗ Relies on GPC Internet but  outside vendors for firewall  management and security

RESPONSE

##### Body text (outside table regions)

FiberFirst Metronet Allo Google Fiber

BATTLE CARD Omaha Metro competitors

FIBERFIRST View Service Area

Strengths

Weaknesses

How We Win

ÔÇ║ Strategic market entry - Entering  underserved areas of the Omaha Metro  where no fiber competition exist

ÔÇ║ Poor customer experience ranging  from staff untrained with customer  interactions to incomplete installs for  multi-product customers

ÔÇ║ We employ local employees to help  support and maintain business services

ÔÇ║ Our fiber network has built-in  redundancy for business continuity

ÔÇ║ Local community presence by  sponsoring revamped ballfields and  advertising with Omaha Magazine

ÔÇ║ Limited redundancy nationwide,  meaning a fiber cut anywhere could  mean an outage everywhere

ÔÇ║ We are more established in the Omaha  market, with a strong reputation as  industry experts providing excellent  customer experience backed by 110+  years of experience

ÔÇ║ Quick turnaround on costing and  construction - often within 2 weeks

ÔÇ║ No physical storefronts or customer  support in or around the Omaha area

ÔÇ║ We have a diverse, 100% referenceable  customer base

METRONET View Service Area

Strengths

Weaknesses

How We Win

ÔÇ║ 100% fiber network offering symmetrical  speeds

ÔÇ║ Inconsistent customer support  reliability with long hold times, billing  inconsistencies or poor communication  during outages or technical issues

ÔÇ║ 24x7 technical and Network Operations  Center support

ÔÇ║ Lower advertised price points than GPC  business Internet services

ÔÇ║ Active involvement in organizations  across the Omaha Metro, and entire  footprint, to build relationships and our  brand

ÔÇ║ While Metronet is not a new company,  they are new to the Omaha metro; they  have limited brand recognition and  focus here

ÔÇ║ Nationwide provider with nationwide  recognition, especially after being  purchased by T-Mobile

ÔÇ║ Gained a strong reputation as industry  experts providing an excellent  experience to our business customers  backed by customer testimonials and a  100% referenceable customer base

ÔÇ║ Their network is built quickly and  cheaply, often using aerial fiber

T-Mobile and Metronet  Connection

ÔÇ║ Limited long-term investment in local  communities

ÔÇ║ High-capacity network strategically  designed, built and upgraded, using  mostly buried fiber (~98%) in the Omaha  Metro

T-Mobile has purchased Metronet and  plans to operate the residential side  of their business under the T-Mobile  brand. They plan to operate the B2B  side under the Metronet name.

ÔÇ║ While the T-Mobile acquisition provides  larger financial backing to expand, it  could mean instability as the business  side is still branded Metronet

ÔÇ║ We focus on building relationships with  our customers and partners

ALLO View Service Area

Strengths

Weaknesses

How We Win

ÔÇ║ No business contracts

ÔÇ║ Pocketed availability in Omaha city  limits - we won a deal back because Allo  couldnÔÇÖt get to the customer

ÔÇ║ Resources

ÔÇ║ Multiple storefronts in and around the  Omaha Metro

ÔÇ║ Capable of service Omaha businesses  within 500 feet of our fiber with more  streamlined installations

ÔÇ║ Do not fully own their network - lease  fiber from GPC and others

ÔÇ║ Lower advertised price points than GPC  business Internet services

ÔÇ║ GPC contracts are a benefit to customers  to lock rates in, meaning no Internet rate  increases for the length of the term

ÔÇ║ Recently held voluntary layoffs due to  funding issues

ÔÇ║ Completed full market buildouts outside  of Omaha - Fremont, Seward, Kearney

ÔÇ║ Reputation for raising their rates on a  regular basis

GOOGLE FIBER View Service Area

Weaknesses

How We Win

Strengths

ÔÇ║ Simplified bandwidth and pricing  options - 2 tiers only

ÔÇ║ Limited business services - not a full- service provider

ÔÇ║ Provide a full suite of services, one bill  and one point of contact

ÔÇ║ Wi-Fi is included with the installation

ÔÇ║ No local support presence - everything is  handled remotely or automated

ÔÇ║ Able to support more complex business  needs such as healthcare organizations  and multi-site businesses

ÔÇ║ Lower advertised price points than GPC  business Internet services

ÔÇ║ Not ideal for businesses with complex  needs (DDoS, SD-WAN, etc.)

ÔÇ║ Solutions for mom-and-pop  businesses that have lower bandwidth  requirements - youÔÇÖre not paying for  what you donÔÇÖt need

ÔÇ║ Only two tiers - smaller businesses do  not need 1 Gig and could do with less at  a lesser price point

ÔÇ║ Custom solutions to fit the need of every  business and industry

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H

OVERVIEW

WHAT ARE WE ANNOUCING?

Our New 5G Wireless Internet Services

1. Wireless Internet Backup ÔÇô 5G
2. Wireless Internet Broadband ÔÇô 5G

ÔÇó  Wireless Internet Backup

ÔÇó Provides a wireless backup for GPC wireline Internet access ÔÇô SIA, DIA

ÔÇó Only active during outage on primary line ÔÇô fails over and fails back, automatically

ÔÇó New 5G Speeds are 4-5 times faster than 4G LTE

ÔÇó  Wireless Internet Broadband

ÔÇó Full-time wireless Internet access

ÔÇó Use Case:  Temporary service while waiting for fiber.

ÔÇó Alternate Use Case:  Redundant Internet Access ÔÇô Governments, Healthcare, Banks

Confidential        ┬ę 2025 Great Plains Communications

#### Page 3 / 19 ÔÇö `page-003.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H

OVERVIEW

WHAT ARE WE ANNOUCING?

How fast is 5G?

ÔÇó  Speeds can range from ~100Mbps to >200Mbps

ÔÇó  Compare ÔÇô LTE Average Range is 20-25Mbps

ÔÇó  Baseline 5G is 4 ÔÇô 5 times faster than LTE, and could be higherÔÇŽ

ÔÇó Verizon is rate limited to 100Mbps on our plan

ÔÇó AT&T tested at 239 Mbps down, 22Mbps up.

ÔÇó Remember ÔÇô Wireless is not like fiber ÔÇô not symmetrical and every location is different!

ÔÇó  Wireless bandwidth is always best effort -  speeds can vary by carrier, location, signal strength

and quality, proximity to cell tower and other environmental factors. The underlying wireless carrier

may temporarily slow data speeds if network is busy.

Confidential        ┬ę 2025 Great Plains Communications

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H

OVERVIEW

Key Features

Internet Backup Key Selling Features

ÔÇó Automatic failover to Verizon or AT&T 5G wireless networks

ÔÇó Automatic reversion to the primary Internet connection once service is restored

ÔÇó 5G Wireless bandwidth speeds

ÔÇó Professional configuration and installation of the Cradlepoint wireless router

ÔÇó Optional backup battery with up to 8 hours of run time

ÔÇó Unlimited data usage and on full time Broadband

ÔÇó After Failover to the cellular wireless network, GPC will limit data rates as follows:

ÔÇó After 12GB of daily usage, data speeds are up to 50Mbps

ÔÇó After 20GB of daily usage, data speeds are up to 25Mbps

ÔÇó After 30GB or daily usage, data speeds are up to 3Mbps

ÔÇó Note:  The underlying wireless carrier may temporarily slow data speeds if network is busy.

Confidential        ┬ę 2025 Great Plains Communications

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H

OVERVIEW

TARGET MARKETS

Customer Pain Points

Target Markets ÔÇô Small Business Examples

1. Small businesses loose business when the Internet

ÔÇó Healthcare

goes down and they cannot provide services or sales.

ÔÇó Doctors offices, clinics and pharmacies need on-line

access to patient records, e-fax and e-prescribing.

1. Coffee shop patrons require reliable Internet or they will

take their business elsewhere.

ÔÇó Retail

1. Payment card processing require reliable Internet

ÔÇó Retail Stores and restaurants need Internet access for

access or sales cannot be completed.

POS terminals and to process credit card orders.

1. Without on-line access, companies that rely on cloud

ÔÇó Services

services will find employees cannot do their jobs.

ÔÇó As applications move to the cloud, financial, Legal,

Accounting, Insurance services need reliable Internet.

1. Redundant landline access is too expensive and too

complex for most small and medium sized businesses.

ÔÇó Local/City Government

ÔÇó Broadband Internet with network/carrier redundancy

OVERVIEW

HOW DOES IT WORK?

Installation

ÔÇó Cradlepoint routers come with dual 5G SIMs from

Verizon and AT&T

ÔÇó During installation - Special software automatically

measures signal Strength and Quality for each

carrier.

ÔÇó SIM with best signal is activated, other deactivated

ÔÇó Result ÔÇô Very high install success rate*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

OVERVIEW

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H HOW DOES IT WORK?

Wireless Backup 5G - Automatic Failover

ÔÇó Cradlepoint Monitors flow of traffic to  the Internet

ÔÇó If an interruption of Internet traffic is  detected, router will automatically  switch over to wireless LTE.

ÔÇó Failover takes around one minute.

ÔÇó Router will automatically switch back  to landline when service is restored.

*Note:  Static IP addresses will work on the GPC landline Internet, but not during failover to the wireless network.

Confidential        ┬ę 2025 Great Plains Communications

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H

OVERVIEW

HOW DOES IT WORK?

Wireless Internet Broadband

ÔÇó Cradlepoint forwards traffic from  customer switch/router to Verizon or  AT&T 5G network.

ÔÇó You must pre-qualify 5G availability on  the carrier Website.

ÔÇó No static IP on Wireless Internet  Broadband ÔÇô IP provided by wireless  carrier using DHCP, so servers hosted  on prem will not be reachable from the  Internet.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Get the best of Intermedia Ascend  and Microsoft Teams - together Intermedia Ascend   with  Microsoft Teams

Designed for businesses who have oriented their collaboration around Teams, yet  need enterprise-grade PBX, contact center capabilities, SMS, and/or a desk phone.

The right apps for the right services :  Users work within Teams for chat, meetings,  and files, and use Ascend  desktop and  mobile apps for all phone-related services.

SINGLE SIGN ON

Ascend  for  Communications

Teams  for  Collaboration

Easy-to-use:   Ascend  collaboration features  (chat, meetings, and file sharing) are  disabled, so users arenÔÇÖt faced with  confusing options.

Advanced capabilities:  In addition  to communicating through enterprise-grade  cloud PBX, organizations have access to  Business SMS, Call Center and CRM  integrations thru Ascend  at no additional  cost.

Single sign-on:  Users sign into Ascend   with their M365 credentials.

USE Ascend  FOR  Native Phone, Contact Center,  SMS, Presence and Integrations

USE TEAMS FOR  Chat, meetings, files

##### Body text (outside table regions)

For businesses looking to add reliable, award-winning PBX into their Microsoft Teams application. Intermedia Ascend   for  Microsoft Teams

Designed to boost the power and functionality of Microsoft Teams through the enterprise-grade

Cloud PBX of Intermedia Ascend .

Seamless user experience :  One app for both collaboration  and voice means users manage calls through the native  Teams application

Advanced capabilities :  Leverage Ascend ÔÇÖs enterprise- grade calling features, including auto attendant, call  queuing, automatic company-wide call recording, and  more within the Teams applications.

Familiar Tools :  Working 100% within Microsoft Teams  applications, users can keep their Ascend  phone numbers,  extensions and call flows, no additional training or  hardware required

Archiving Discovery Questions

Opening the Prospect for Their Archiving Needs

Have you ever had the  need to search/ review  your communication data?

How do you search and  retrieve communication  today?

How long does it take  to search your  communications?

General  Questions:

Some Businesses ÔÇśMust HaveÔÇÖ

All Businesses Should Have

Financial Services/ Healthcare/ Legal/ Energy/ Pharma

Have you ever had to deal with customer or  internal disputes? How would you rate your ability  to adhere to compliance and  governance obligations for  existing information?

Improve Sales  Performance and  Customer Experience

How long do you need to retain  communication data? Are there  any specific storage  requirements (e.g. WORM)?

How efficiently can you access customer/ sales  calls? Do you have holistic view over all  customer interactions  (including any SMS &  chat) ?

keeping Monitoring

Record-

Do you currently leverage existing company  communication knowledge? Do you see any  value in this data?

How many communication and  collaboration platforms do you  have in your organization? Do you  have to preserve and monitor all?

How effectively do you, search,  retrieve, access and export the  communication data today?

Better Knowledge  Transfer

& Audits Mitigate

How do you deal with continuation once an  employee leaves an organization?

Better Support for  eDiscovery,  Legal Requests

How often are you faced with eDiscovery,  investigation or litigation requests?

Is your communication securely  backed up? Do you have ÔÇśaccess  controlsÔÇÖ and ÔÇśgranular  permissionsÔÇÖ in place?

Risk

#### Page 64 / 125 ÔÇö `page-064.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Sales Scoping QuestionÔÇÖs

ÔÇó Do you need to retain communication data? Can it help your day-to-day operations?

This question will enable us to open up and understand which options and additional module sets would be suitable for the  prospect/ client. E.g. Compliance teams may require the Compliance module to help with its processes.

ÔÇó Retention period?

Understand client's retention requirements.

ÔÇó Number of Ascend  / Ascend  Users?

This will help you understand the size of the deal and therefore associated pricing.

ÔÇó Storage type? (Read-Only & WORM)

Does the client require a particular standard for storage? E.g. WORM for immutable storage for regulatory purposes?

ÔÇó Data residency? Where would the client like to hold their data?

Understand which location clients would like their data to be held. Ascend  Archiving follows GCP (Google Cloud Platform)  locations, including the US, Canada, UK, Europe, and APAC.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Archiving Pricing & Packaging

Notes: 1. $/ User License/ Month  Pricing is driven by retention period  tiers and is presented as cost per  license per month.

1. ÔÇśAll or NothingÔÇÖ Licensing Archiving licenses must be equal to  the number of Ascend  licenses. No  ÔÇśsubsetÔÇÖ selection is available yet.

If a company has 10 Ascend  user licenses,  the client requires 10 Ascend  Archiving  licenses.

1. Unlimited Storage* A generous fair usage, for the rare/  unlikely event an account exceeds it.
2. Custom Retention Options Retention is not limited to only the  displayed SKUs.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Pricing FAQÔÇÖs

ÔÇó Can a customer upgrade/downgrade plans?  Yes, in Control Panel.

ÔÇó Can a customer capture data & pay for only a subset of their users?  No ÔÇô but speak to us.

ÔÇó If a user leaves (User A), and is replaced by another user (User B); will you still retain user AÔÇÖs data?

Yes, all usersÔÇÖ (old and new) communications will still be stored in the archive

ÔÇó Can a customer elect to not capture SMS/Chat/Voice?  Yes ÔÇô support ticket.

ÔÇó Will Contact Center / Email (when integrated later in 2024) be included in the price?  No ÔÇô these services

will be an additional cost.

ÔÇó What data residency options are available?  USA, Canada, UK ÔÇô in-region data centers.

ÔÇó Is WORM (tamper-proof storage) an additional cost?  No ÔÇô but needs support ticket to enable.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

5 Question Qualifying Sequence

How do your customer reach you today? How do you reach your customers? ÔÇó Toll-free, local number, email chat social, SMS, fax, web forms, apps.. What are they not using?

What person or team talks the most to your customers? ÔÇó Who is responsible for care/sales/support? This is likely who you need to talk with.

What do they do (sales/support/billing)? Who calls who? ÔÇó Are they able to work remotely, who manages the team and makes decisions?

How do you measure success? How valuable are your calls? What tools are you using currently? ÔÇó Look at telephone, how calls are being tracked, reporting, recordedÔÇŽ also as about CRMÔÇÖs or other systems

What are your customers saying about you? Is there someone at the company that is responsible  for responding to negative reviews? ÔÇó Tread lightly but this can shape our discussion with them and illuminate the market consensus around  current CX .

| QUICK ACCESS            | ÔÇó Do you know how many calls you miss a day? ÔÇó How long do customers wait on hold or how long to get to the right person?                                                       |

Stats Make It Real

TEXTING

ÔÇó  90% of text messages are read in the first three minutes. ÔÇó  Texting has a 45% response rate compared to 6% of emails. ÔÇó  75% of people wonÔÇÖt answer a call from an unknown number.

CHAT

ÔÇó  Customers who use live chat, spend up to 60% more per purchase vs others. ÔÇó  Live chat costs companies 15-33% less compared to phone support. ÔÇó  Live chat is the number one service choice for shoppers between 18-49 years old.

NOTIFICATIONS

ÔÇó  Healthcare providers lose $200 for each no show on average. ÔÇó  75% of millennials think that text is a helpful way to receive reminders.

Practice Scenario #1: SD Midship Credit Union

Business: SD Midship Credit Union Industry: Banking/Finance Contact: The office manager came across an online ad and read your website. He is not technically proficient  and is conducting research on options for his business.

Context:  Looking for a call center solution to help its overworked 15 front-desk workers handle an increasing load of inbound  calls from its members.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó The front-desk employees are stressed.  ÔÇó Too many calls coming in, long wait times, leading to frustrated customers.  ÔÇó Major concern that customer satisfaction ratings seem to be starting to slip. ÔÇó Adding new staff may pose challenges as office space is maxed out. ÔÇó The executive management team likes their phones and is wary of introducing any additional risk into business  operations.

Current Solution: Old Avaya-based on-premises system.

Practice Scenario #2: South Denver Dental Clinic

Business: South Denver Dental Clinic Industry: Dental/Healthcare Contact: An IT manager at a 25-employee Colorado-based dental practice

Context: IT Manager, with very little knowledge of phones systems, is conducting a review of suppliers for a new phone system  to support the dental office. ItÔÇÖs down to Intermedia and RingCentral. Three receptionists at the front desk juggle to  check-in patients while the phone seems to ring off the hook. Often their practice is to pick up and place the caller  on hold until they can get back to them. You want to introduce Contact Center, but you also donÔÇÖt want to lose the  Ascend  deal with too many complications. Dentists (the owners) donÔÇÖt want to deal with any added costs or  complexit y.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó Older phone system offers limited functionality and low voice quality. ÔÇó Manual complexity and cost of administering the phone system when new employees arrive, or others leave. ÔÇó Stressed out front-desk employees who know that patient-service is suffering.  ÔÇó There is also a high percentage of patients who show up late or miss appointments altogether.

Current Solution: On prem phone system installed in 1986.

Practice Scenario #3: Jacob & Marley Real Estate Agency

Business: Jacob & Marley Real Estate Industry: Real Estate/Professional Services Contact: A seasoned IT manager for one of IowaÔÇÖs top real estate agencies with 125 employees

Context: Upgrading their companyÔÇÖs on-premises phone system and opening two new offices with many agents on the road. 2-3 business stakeholders will join the RFP and review process. Looking for functionality that will give their real  estate firm the competitive edge when it comes to customer service.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó Tipping point with cost and complexity of on-prem phone system with company growth. ÔÇó IT frustration to administer the expensive and complex solution with new buildings and an increasingly mobile  workforce. ÔÇó Customers are adopting new changes such as chat on the website, SMS and phone. ÔÇó Agents in the field donÔÇÖt like having to use their personal phones to conduct business.

Current Solution: Toshiba on premise phone system with no good solution to upgrade.

Practice Scenario #4: Stairway to Haggis Pub

Business: Stairway to Haggis Pub Industry: Restaurant Contact: The owner (not IT professional but technically savvy) of a popular Scottish Pub on Manhattan Beach in  Los Angeles is upgrading his customer experience

Context: Word of mouth is important in his business, and he wants to offer an efficient experience along with great food, drink, and  atmosphere. Reservations can come in via phone call, website, or email. With long wait times on Friday and Saturday nights,  he wants to ask patrons to provide their cell number to receive a text when the next table is ready. He intends to look at a  couple of contenders, not only to find the right solution but also to learn about what functionality is available.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó An increasingly sophisticated audience and many other dining establishments upgrading infrastructure. ÔÇó A hard-working crew who needs to handle inbound calls, texts, and emails, while welcoming patrons at the front door. ÔÇó No insight into how many calls come in, how long patrons are waiting on hold, or how many abandon the call altogether. ÔÇó The restaurant business runs on tight margins. There is no desire for an excessively high-cost solution.

Current Solution: A basic phone solution from Comcast Business

Practice Scenario #5: Fircrest East Automotive

Business: Fircrest East Automotive Industry: Automotive Contact: The service manager at Fircrest East Automotive who manages 12 service reps

Context:  The service manager for a large Automotive dealer with a high volume of customers to process. Tired of the nickel  and diming he gets from RingCentral and is ready for a fair deal. Service manager is working with IT consultant to  help answer technical questions and admin the new system.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó Getting nickeled and dimed with every conversation. ÔÇó A high volume of inbound inquiries to processÔÇöand to do so with the highest level of customer service.   ÔÇó Corporate independently surveys customers from each of the franchises and applies ratings that can affect  business.   ÔÇó A mobile workforce. Service professionals are either at the front service desk, on the showroom floor or out in  the back lot where repairs are taking place. ÔÇó The environment is dynamic, and customers want to be routed quickly to whomever is handling their account to  find out about repairs, costs, and timing of pick-up.

Current Solution: A cloud-based phone solution

Practice Scenario #6: Harley & Hutch Legal

Business: Harley & Hutch Legal Industry: Legal Contact: Office Manager

Context: Two law offices are merging and want to consolidate their communications ÔÇô one has Intermedia, the other has 8x8.  The new law firm plans to launch a major campaign to drum up business and expects a high volume of inbound calls as  a result. They arenÔÇÖt interested in industry jargon.

Prospect Pain Points: ÔÇó Personal injury is a competitive field. Inquiries come in day and night ÔÇô callers are contacting the firm because they  are in trouble and scared. They want immediate help. ÔÇó The firm moves fast ÔÇô associates are under pressure to find cases that are worthwhile for the firm to take on and  then they are up against insurance companies, district attorneys, large corporations and others to win the case.   Communication mostly takes place by phone.   ÔÇó They expect to see a groundswell of inbound queries. They want those queries addressed because they want to  grow aggressively. They also want to understand the ROI of their campaign.

Current Solution: Two different solutions ÔÇô Intermedia and 8x8

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Prospecting Tip: Ask For Their Toll-Free Bill

High Toll-Free Users

$500.00 (13,000 toll free minutes/month * $0.03)  = ~5 contact center agents

$750.00 (25,000 toll free minutes/month * $0.03) = ~10 contact center agents

$1,875.00 (62,500 toll free minutes/month * $0.03) = ~25 contact center agents

Prospecting Tip: Ask For Their Phone Bill

Big PRI/SIP Channels

o  See if their PRI/SIP channels outnumber handsets

o  ItÔÇÖs likely theyÔÇÖre using queues

Example

o  50 handsets deployed at a location, but they only have 3 PRIs

o  Each ACD user should have at least two channels provisioned

o  1 for the connected caller, 1 for the queued caller

Setting For Success:  Discovery Calls and   Customizing Your  Demo

Eoin ÔÇťOwenÔÇŁ Bathe and Mitch  ÔÇťMitchÔÇŁ Adams Sr. Service Provider Channel Managers Monday, April 13, 2026

#### Page 2 / 55 ÔÇö `page-002.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

The Discovery Call

30 to 60 Minutes

Discovery Call Structure:

1. Pre-Call
2. Why Are We Here(The problem)
3. The Customer Experience
4. The Agent Experience
5. The Management Experience
6. Technical Issues/Concerns
7. Who Will Be At The Demo
8. Anything Else? What Would You Like To See?

2

#### Page 3 / 55 ÔÇö `page-003.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Discovery

Pre-Call

#### Page 4 / 55 ÔÇö `page-004.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

The Discovery Call

Know the company and who you are speaking with

1. Visit their website; ÔÇťContact UsÔÇŁ page
2. Visit their ÔÇťAbout UsÔÇŁ Page

o Get a sense of the CX (customer experience)

o Looking for the person responsible  for ÔÇťcustomer successÔÇŁ ÔÇô Read Bio  (if available) and pick out some  words

o Toll Free, Local Numbers, Email, Web Forms, Chat  Button, Fax

o The more of the above, the more complex to  manage

1. Look for online reviews on them
2. Visit that personÔÇÖs Linkedin Page (if they  have one)

o Get a sense of the CX reviews ÔÇô good or bad

o Looking for areas of responsibility  ÔÇô pick out more key words

o See the types of compliments or complaints people  are leaving

o Think of features that can assist  in their responsibilities

o Have a way to link our features / benefits to solving  those problems

4

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Discovery

THE PROBLEM

#### Page 6 / 55 ÔÇö `page-006.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

The Discovery Call

Why Are We Here?

ÔÇó Can you tell me of the event for the organization to start looking for a new system?

ÔÇó What happened?

ÔÇó What is driving your interest in exploring solutions currently?

We are looking to  capture the compelling event  and to qualify if the need is great enough for the prospect to

trigger a change.

Changing contact center is not a trivial undertaking.

Possible answers:

New Management; End of Life PBX/Prem; New Parent; New Products; Growth/Shrinkage; Staffing Issues; Expiring  Contacts; New LocationsÔÇŽ

6

#### Page 7 / 55 ÔÇö `page-007.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Discovery

CUSTOMER EX

#### Page 8 / 55 ÔÇö `page-008.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

The Discovery Call

The Customer Experience

ÔÇó Who are your clients? ÔÇó How can customers/patients/employees reach you today? ÔÇó How many toll-free numbers do you have? ÔÇó How many calls do you receive on a monthly basis? ÔÇó What is my experience like as a customer calling in?

ÔÇó Are there any repetitive calls that could be addressed  automatically? (Self Service)

ÔÇó Examples: Order status, making payments ÔÇó Are all clients treated equally when they call-in or do they  some receive priority?

ÔÇó Chat

ÔÇó Are calls answered right away? Or is there a wait  time?

ÔÇó Email

ÔÇó SMS ÔÇó What is the value of a customer interaction?

ÔÇó Are clients complaining of any issues?

ÔÇó Long wait times?

ÔÇó What is the cost of a customer interaction? ÔÇó Is there any data or reports you can share with me regarding  customer interactions? ÔÇó What can we do to make your customers life easier?

ÔÇó Abandoned calls?

ÔÇó Calls not reaching the right agents?

ÔÇó Calls going to voicemail? ÔÇó What are the reasons for callers calling?

##### Body text (outside table regions)

AGENT EX

#### Page 10 / 55 ÔÇö `page-010.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

The Discovery Call

The Agent Experience

ÔÇó What applications am I using today to manage  customer interactions?

ÔÇó Do agents work from home, remote offices or head  office?

ÔÇó Email, SMS, Chat, CRM, Billing?

ÔÇó How many at each location and at any one  time?

ÔÇó What's my workflow? (open ended question on purpose)

ÔÇó Multiple shifts?

ÔÇó How are your agents/staff grouped?

ÔÇó Are there any user actions that you feel can be  automated?

ÔÇó By functional area? By geography? By  product? By skillset?

ÔÇó How long does it take to onboard a new agent?

ÔÇó As an agent do I make outbound calls?

ÔÇó What can we do to make the agents life easier?

ÔÇó What is the nature of the calls? What is the  objective?

The Management Experience

##### Body text (outside table regions)

CCaaS Solution Outcomes ÔÇô The WHY

Focus on your business objectives i.e. why do people care? WeÔÇÖre solving for  pain, so understand why it hurtsÔÇŽ

ÔÇó  Work from Anywhere!!!!  ÔÇó  Reduce Costs ÔÇô Spend less money/increase ROI ÔÇó  Increase Revenue ÔÇô Make more money (Humans are the most expensive

part) ÔÇó  Create/Increase Customer Loyalty/Experience ÔÇó  Ensure Industry Regulation and Company Policy ÔÇó  End of Life/Deprecation/Maintenance Agreements ÔÇó  Agent Experience ÔÇô Ease of use (Tying systems together)

#### Page 35 / 55 ÔÇö `page-035.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Work From Anywhere ÔÇô The WHY

ÔÇó No loss of valuable data:

With our reports/dashboards, regardless of where the agents are, you will  maintain visibility.

ÔÇó Can maintain excellent customer experience:

With in-queue treatment, custom messaging, announcements, skills based  routing of remote agents, your customer experience will remain steadfast  and there will be no impact. Callers will not be aware your agents are  working remotely

ÔÇó No degradation in employee visibility:

With the live monitor, executive & metric dashboards, you will still have  whole insight into your agents behaviors and activities throughout the day.  No need to be physically close to your agents.

ÔÇó You still remain open and make money!

Whilst you may have to close your physical offices, your phone lines  remain open and busier than ever!

35

#### Page 36 / 55 ÔÇö `page-036.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Work From Anywhere ÔÇô The WHY

ÔÇó  Fewer Logistical Barriers to Hiring/Staffing

Agents or new hires are geographically challenged in the short term  or long term? No problem, agents can access the CC from  ANYWHERE!

ÔÇó  Maintain Operational Efficiency

Reduce abandoned called, rolled called calls, missed calls, reduce  handle times and improve service level etc.

ÔÇó  Use Any Phone!!

GPC Customer Network Monitoring Portal

#### Page 2 / 10 ÔÇö `page-002.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT  P L A IN S  C O M M U N IC AT IO NS

Customer Network Monitoring Portal

ÔÇó What is it?

ÔÇó A customer-facing network monitoring portal for DIA and Ethernet

ÔÇó What Does It Do?

ÔÇó The CNM Portal provides real time monitoring of customer circuits and network traffic.

ÔÇó The portal shows circuit status (Green, Yellow, Red, etc.) and packet performance

metrics such as bandwidth utilization, packet loss, delay and jitter.

ÔÇó Customers can create scheduled or on-demand reports showing historic trends

hourly, daily, weekly and monthly.

ÔÇó Who Should Get it?

ÔÇó Any business or organization large enough to have an IT manager or staff capable of

understanding network performance analysis.

#### Page 3 / 10 ÔÇö `page-003.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT  P L A IN S  C O M M U N IC AT IO NS

Customer Network Monitoring Portal

ÔÇó  Why Do Customers Need It?

ÔÇó Application performance is a top concern for enterprises and service providers.

ÔÇó Network issues that can impact application performance can be hard to identify.

ÔÇó The portal enables customers to find the root causes of application performance

issues.

ÔÇó  How Much Does It Cost?

ÔÇó There is no charge for the Network Monitoring Portal.

ÔÇó It does have a $7 monthly cost to GPC.

ÔÇó The Portal can be used as a closing tool for new DIA or Ethernet accounts.

ÔÇó The Portal can be provided to existing DIA or Ethernet customers with renewal or

upgrade.

ÔÇó Minimum term 12 months, concurrent with underlying service.

#### Page 4 / 10 ÔÇö `page-004.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT  P L A IN S  C O M M U N IC AT IO NS

Customer Network Monitoring Portal

Ordering Process if ordered with:

1. New/Upgrade/Renewing Ethernet/DIA Service

ÔÇó Add portal product in Salesforce order as part of the service order.

ÔÇó There is a separate product in SF for the Network Monitoring Portal.

ÔÇó One portal order per account, not per circuit.  Portal will be applied to all circuits unless otherwise

specified in order notes.

ÔÇó Network Engineering will set customer up with login credentials and basic monthly

reports that they can customize.

1. Existing Ethernet customers with no other product changes do not need a new order in

Salesforce.

ÔÇó Send an email to Business Care (cc Product) with the following information:

ÔÇó Customer name, account number, circuit ID, customer admin contact.

ÔÇó Business care will task Network Engineering to create the account and inform

customer of login credentials.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT PL AI NS CO M M U NIC ATIO NS

WHAT IS CLOUD COMPUTING

Cloud Characteristics

ÔÇó  On-demand availability

Cloud computing is the on-demand availability of computer  system resources like data storage, applications, and computing  power, that are accessed via the Internet or direct connectivity to  another providerÔÇÖs shared cloud computing infrastructure.

ÔÇó  Easily Accessed

ÔÇó  Multi-tenant resource pooling

ÔÇó  Ability to scale rapidly

ÔÇó  Usage Analytics

Cloud vs. On-premise:

ÔÇó  In simple terms, difference between on-premise and cloud is

essentially where your hardware, application and software  reside.

ÔÇó  On-premise means that a company keeps all of this IT

infrastructure onsite, which is either managed by themselves or a  third-party.  ÔÇó  With the Cloud it means that it is housed offsite with someone

else responsible for monitoring and maintaining it.

#### Page 12 / 26 ÔÇö `page-012.png`

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT  P L A IN S  C O M M U N IC AT IO NS

LAYER 2 VS THE PUBLIC INTERNET

ÔŚĆ AWS, Azure, and GCP can be reached via the public Internet

ÔŚĆ Megaport offers Private (Layer 2) connectivity which offers the following benefits:

ÔŚő More reliable performance vs Internet VPN

ÔŚĆ Consistent latency over a private network mitigates jitter.

ÔŚő Enhanced Security Posture through the following:

ÔŚĆ Removes the vulnerability of DDoS attacks

ÔŚĆ Removes the possibility of malicious spoofing and hijacks

ÔŚĆ Customers can apply encryption over these private layer 2 connections for added security

ÔŚő Reduction in egress fees vs Internet VPN

ÔŚĆ AWS,  Azure and GCP egress charges over Direct Connect, ExpressRoute and Partner Interconnect are approximately 70% discounted  over the egress charges applied to  an Internet VPN. The cloud providers offer these discounted rates as an incentive for customers to  use the layer 2 connectivity services.

#### Page 13 / 26 ÔÇö `page-013.png`

##### Tables (detected)

*None detected on this page.*

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT PL AI NS CO M M U NIC ATIO NS

DEDICATED VS. PARTNER

ÔŚĆ AWS, Azure, and GCP all offer dedicated interconnection

ÔŚő Physical Ethernet connection associated with a single cloud provider. Customer  has to request connection  directly from the cloud provider and work to have cross connects run/configured

ÔŚő Larger bandwidth requirements for dedicated interconnection (i.e. higher costs). For Azure a customer must  subscribe to either dual 10G ports or dual 100G ports.

ÔŚő Location specific. A  customer must get to a cloud provider edge location. There are a fewer number of these  locations vs the number of Megaport enabled locations.

ÔŚĆ Megaport is a connectivity partner with the major cloud providers

ÔŚő Greater reach. You can connect from hundreds of Megaport enabled data centers.

ÔŚő Ability to deploy connections from upto 10Gbps

ÔŚő Ability to connect to multiple cloud providers from a single connection to Megaport

#### Page 14 / 26 ÔÇö `page-014.png`

##### Tables (detected)

##### Body text (outside table regions)

G RE AT  P L A IN S  C O M M U N IC AT IO NS

CLOUD CONNECTIVITY TCO

ÔŚĆ Megaport Components (Billed to GPC)

ÔŚő Access

Ôľá Megaport (a 1Gbps or 10Gbps NNI between the partner and Megaport)

ÔŚő Private Connections (VXCs/VLANs)

ÔŚĆ CSP Components (Billed to the owner of cloud resources, typically the end-user)

ÔŚő Virtual Port Charge- Hourly charge based off of capacity subscribed to

ÔŚő Data Transfer- Fees for egress over the Layer 2 connection

G RE AT PL AI NS CO M M U NIC ATIO NS

PRODUCT OVERVIEW

FEATURES & BENEFITS

ÔÇó  Runs over our private fiber Ethernet

network, customer traffic to and from  the Cloud does not pass through the  public Internet

GPC Cloud Connect provides a layer 2 Ethernet (EVPL/ELAN) connection  from an end-user location to a network-to-network interface (NNI) with  Megaport where we have multiple NNIs in geo-diverse locations. Megaport  is a cloud integrator with direct connections to multiple cloud service  providers (CSP) including Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure,  Google, Oracle, IBM, SAP, Nutanix, Salesforce and Alibaba. GPC is  providing the connectivity to the cloud, NOT the cloud service itself.

ÔÇó  Provides a fast, flexible and secure

direct connection to multiple leading  Cloud and Network Providers on our  cloud ecosystem

ÔÇó  Establishes connectivity without

needing to own or manage a physical  network

ÔÇó  Keeps traffic secure when connecting

between metros and regions

ÔÇó  Provides multi-region redundancy by

connecting to multiple cloud regions  from a single interconnection point

ÔÇó  Provides direct connections with

speeds up to 10 Gbps

Note: Secondary Cloud Connect  is a  virtual circuit utilizing the same  Megaport interconnect port  (Omaha, Chicago, or Denver) as the  primary and serves only as backup.  Customer would not utilize more  bandwidth than what is subscribed  to in the primary (i.e. primary 1Gb  & secondary 1Gb but total usage  would be limited to 1Gb).

PRICING ELEMENTS

1. ELINE-EVPL or ELAN | 50Mb ÔÇô 10Gb

E-LINE PRODUCT OVERVIEW

The E-Line product consists of two types of components:

ÔÇó The User Network Interface (UNI) at each end ÔÇó The Ethernet Virtual Connection (EVC)

GR EAT P L A IN S CO MMU N ICATIO N S

E-LINE | EPL

Ethernet Private Line  (EPL) ÔÇô This service type is a point-to-point Port- based service that is intended to be highly transparent in the sense that  service frames that are sent are delivered with as few modifications as  possible.  Traffic within these frames are managed by the customer.

GR EAT P L A IN S CO MMU N ICATIO N S

E-LAN PRODUCT OVERVIEW

Ethernet Local Area Network  (E-LAN)  -  An E-LAN is a multipoint-to-multipoint  service that connects a number of UNIs (2 or more) providing full mesh  connectivity for those sites. Each UNI can communicate with any other UNI that  is connected to that Ethernet service.

| GR E AT P L A I NS COM M U NI CATI ONS CLASS OF SERVICE (CoS) Class of Service Defined : ÔÇó Ethernet Class of Service (CoS) provides a standard solution for a higher level of service commitment from Great Plains Communications (GPC) to our customers. CoS offers three service levels in addition to standard traffic for Ethernet Private Line (EPL), Ethernet Virtual Private Line (EVPL) or Ethernet Local Area Network (ELAN) products. When CoS is selected, network performance must meet the GPC service level objective defined. | Benefits ÔÇó Provides a multi-tiered service commitment for customers needing assurance of higher network performance ÔÇó Offers network analytics that are readily available for ad hoc reporting |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

#### Page 15 / 16 ÔÇö `page-015.png`

##### Tables (detected)

###### Table 1


| CoS Priority Level     | Common Service Usage                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Real-Time**          | Real-time CoS is optimal for low latency, low jitter and low packet loss performance required for voice communications. All managed Voice over IP (VoIP) services should be defaulted to real-time CoS.                                                                                              |
| **Mission Critical**   | Mission critical CoS is intended for applications with high business value, requiring large bandwidth allocations and/or lower latency such as interactive video conferencing, streaming video, credit card transactions and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) applications like SAP or PeopleSoft. |
| **Business Class**     | Business critical data CoS is intended for transactional and interactive data such as email or client/server applications.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| **Standard (default)** | Standard data CoS is for customers who have latency and jitter tolerant data applications.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

Configure Inc. ÔÇô What We Do

We Provide Managed Network and Security Services using common OEM platforms for our Partners to  sell to their Customers.

Configure Inc.  has been a trusted partner for over 25 years with a 98% customer satisfaction rating. What makes Configure Inc. unique is  our commitment to helping our partners grow and build their Managed Security Services business with our customized solutions breadth  of services, flexibility, agility and focus on expertise in emerging technologies.

ÔÇó  From Fortune 100 to SMB Customers, we Design, Deploy and Manage Network and Security Services for our PartnerÔÇÖs

Customers.  We are the ÔÇťProviderÔÇŁ in their MSP program.  We partner with platform resellers (VARS and MSPs), Telcos  and directly with platform OEMs to support large custom deployments and also create scalable ÔÇťbundleÔÇŁ programs for  SMB markets. ÔÇó  We manage over 25,000 endpoints for our Partners ÔÇó  Our Managed Services solutions support all common OEM Network and Security Platforms (we are NOT an OEM).  We

are Managed Service Solutions Experts. ÔÇó  Our pricing model is structured to give partners a profitable recurring revenue stream on Managed Services sold to

their customers . ÔÇó  With a 98%+ Customer Satisfaction Rating, Partners trust Configure Inc. to support their customers

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Network & Security Monitoring and Management

Configure Inc. can provide full end-to-end LAN, WAN and Security infrastructure deployment, monitoring and management.  We can take over an existing  environment, build a solution from the ground up or provide key integration support when implementing changes in an existing environment.

Our US-based Network Operations Center (NOC) and SOC 2-compliant Security Operations Center  (SOC) services include:

ÔÇó Proactive 24x7x365 Monitoring and Notification  of network and security related services.  USA, Near Shore and Far Shore based. SOCÔÇÖs and NOCÔÇÖs based in Dallas and Chicago.

ÔÇó Fault Isolation and Resolution  (Tier 1.5 ,  2 and 3+)

ÔÇó Managed Component Reporting  and  Capacity Usage Reporting

ÔÇó Preventative maintenance and MACDs

ÔÇó Co-management  options

ÔÇó Ebonding, ServiceNow Service bridge, Electronic integration with client tools

ÔÇó SIEM support  with in-house analysts , Incident Response and Threat Intelligence Security  engineers.

| Col1 | G RE AT P L A IN S C O M M U N IC AT IO NS What is a Denial of Service Attack?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ---- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|      | This type of attack is used to disable or block access to customer servers or websites, by sending a flood of traffic or connection requests that overwhelm that capacity of the servers, thereby preventing legitimate users or customers from accessing customer services. **How it works** Hackers secretly insert malware into hundreds or thousands of legitimate computers. At a designated date and time, the malware activates and takes over the computers. These ÔÇśzombieÔÇÖ computers act in unison to attack the target website or servers, by sending repeated connection requests, for example. GPCÔÇÖs DDoS Protection Service triggers an alarm when a sudden spike in traffic at a particular location exceeds a pre-established threshold. The service identifies the IP addresses of the attackers, and blocks traffic from those addresses from reaching the customer site(s) that are under attack. This process of DDoS attack mitigation can take place in a matter of seconds or minutes, so that customers may not even notice a disruption in their services. |

The GPS DDoS Product

We offer DDoS in GPC West already.  How is this different?

ÔÇó  Network Protection:

ÔÇó GPC West has had DDoS protection to protect the GPC network level (10-100Gb levels). This

level of protection may not detect a 50Mb attack impacting a customer with 50Mb of DIA service.

As of 2021 GPC East also has this level of protection.

ÔÇó  Customer Protection:

ÔÇó The new product is provisioned at a customer circuit level and the notifications and mitigation

levels are configured specifically to protect that customer.

ÔÇó Customer now has access to a portal to view network traffic analytics and attacks at anytime.

| Col1 | G RE AT P L A IN S C O M M U N IC AT IO NS The GPS DDoS Product ÔÇó GPC DDoS Protection Comes in two flavors ÔÇó BGP FlowSpec ÔÇó Highly effective against the most common high-bandwidth (volumetric) attacks that overwhelm access networks with a flood of traffic. ÔÇó BGP FlowSpec can detect volumetric attacks and block or drop malicious traffic in minutes, allowing legitimate traffic to pass through. ÔÇó BGP FlowSpec can mitigate most high-volume attacks, but more sophisticated low- bandwidth attacks require more advanced and automated mitigation. ÔÇó Threat Mitigation System (TMS) ÔÇó GPCs Threat Mitigation System provides a second layer of protection against complex attacks targeting specific equipment or applications that are not easily detectable by standard protection mechanisms. ÔÇó During an attack, automated processes use application-layer intelligence to redirect traffic to the TMS ÔÇśscrubberÔÇÖ that surgically isolates and drops malicious traffic while legitimate traffic is forwarded on to the enterprise. |

##### Body text (outside table regions)

Sales Rules of Engagement

Revised 8/5/2025

General Guidelines & Salesforce Protocol

ÔŚĆ  Source of Truth:  Salesforce is the official system of record and will be used to arbitrate any account disputes.   ÔŚĆ  Account & Contact Creation:

ÔŚő  Before creating a new account, always search Salesforce by address and name to prevent duplicates.   ÔŚő  Do not open a second account record for an existing entity.   ÔŚő  Do not add a new contact with a pre-existing email address. Please coordinate with the current account owner.   ÔŚĆ  Account Transfers:  To request moving an account from another Sales Representative, first connect with the current  owner to discuss. Once agreed, email your manager and sales leadership for approval and transfer.   ÔŚĆ  Evolving Rules:  These rules are designed to manage the majority of situations (~80%). Unique scenarios will be  handled on a case-by-case basis by sales management. This document will be updated as the organization evolves.

General Accounts (Non-Strategic)

This section applies to all accounts NOT on the official Strategic Accounts list.

ÔŚĆ  Current Customers:

ÔŚő  An account with a "Customer" status becomes open to be worked by another representative if there is  no  logged activity for 121 days or more.   ÔŚő  It is always best practice to communicate with the current account owner before engaging with one of their  accounts, even if it appears open.   ÔŚĆ  Prospects:

ÔŚő  An account with a "Prospect" status becomes open if there is  no logged activity for 61 days or more.   ÔŚő  You must keep your accounts updated with relevant information, such as construction projects or contract  dates. However, notation alone does not protect an account; consistent contact (at minimum, every 60 days) is  required.   ÔŚĆ  Inbound Leads:

ÔŚő  Any lead assigned to you by sales or marketing leadership can be reassigned if initial contact is not made and  logged within  5 business days.   ÔŚő  This 5-day rule does not apply to leads that you generate yourself through prospecting.

Strategic Accountss

This section applies to the Strategic Accounts team and the official, named account list.

ÔŚĆ  Account Protection:  All accounts on the official Strategic Accounts list are considered protected. These accounts  are not subject to the activity-based rules (e.g., 61 or 121-day rules) outlined below. They are protected by  assignment, not by activity.   ÔŚĆ  E-Rate & USAC Funded Entities:  All entities, including schools, libraries, and hospitals, that currently utilize or are  eligible for E-Rate and/or USAC funding are designated as Strategic Accounts and are protected, regardless of  explicit inclusion on the Strategic Accounts List.  Exception : ENA (Education Networks of America) based in Indiana  will remain under the Out-State TeamÔÇÖs purview.    ÔŚĆ  Account List Management:  The Strategic Accounts list is dynamic and will be reviewed and amended as needed. All  changes to the list will be managed and communicated by the Sales Managers and the VP of Sales. The official list will  be maintained and distributed separately from this document and housed within the ROE repository of the Sales  Sharepoint.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

GPC UNIFIED COMMUNICATIONS  vs. RingCentral and Zoom SALES BATTLE CARD

OBJECTION HANDLING

QUICK PITCH

Is this system for smaller businesses? While most of our customers are small- to medium-sized, our  solution is built to support the voice and collaboration needs of  large enterprises.

Everyone already knows Zoom. Zoom is great for meetings, but our system keeps your team  working in one spot so you donÔÇÖt lose momentum when the  video ends.

While RingCentral is built for giant call centers and Zoom is  built for video calls, GPCÔÇÖs Unified Communications solution  powered by Intermedia is built for your entire business  workflow. We provide you one integrated platform for  voice, video, chat and - unlike them - secure file backup with  antivirus, all backed by local support that actually answers  the phone.

We already use OneDrive. OneDrive is for storage, but our system includes a one- click rollback to save your files if youÔÇÖre ever hit by a virus or  accidental deletion.

RingCentral has more integrations. We have the same major integrations like Outlook and  Salesforce, but ours work right out of the box without needing an  IT expert to set them up.

RingCentralÔÇÖs AI seems better for sales. You shouldnÔÇÖt have to pay $30+ extra per person for AI insights  that weÔÇÖve already included in the standard platform.

Zoom Phone is only $10. That $10 price is ÔÇťpay-per-minuteÔÇŁ, while our solution covers  collaboration tools plus unlimited calling and secure file backup.

DIFFERENTIATORS THAT MATTER

Integrated   Security Most platforms just share files, but we include  full backup and antivirus to stop ransomware  before it starts.

Real Human  Support You get a local support team and a partner  that has won the J.D. Power Award for  customer satisfaction nine years running.

COMPETITOR WEAKNESSES & DISCOVERY QUESTIONS Company Weakness Discovery Questions

Included AI Our AI is built directly into the app to handle  notes and protect private data without the  $30 monthly upcharge others demand.

When a customer calls you back after a text, can your  team see that entire history on one screen without hunting  through different folders?

You have to jump between three different tabs just to see a  single customerÔÇÖs call, text, and voicemail history.

True   Reliability Our solution powered by Intermedia  guarantees 99.999% uptime, which means  youÔÇÖll see less than six minutes of downtime in  an entire year.

RingCentral

If you decided to change providers tomorrow, do you  know exactly how much notice youÔÇÖre required to give to  avoid an automatic renewal?

Their contracts often include aggressive auto-renewal clauses  that make it very difficult to switch if you arenÔÇÖt happy.

Simple   Pricing Our pricing is straightforward and includes  your features, storage, and even phone  options without hidden ÔÇťadd-onÔÇŁ fees.

Beyond basic video calls, how complex are your current  routing and auto-attendant needs, and do you still rely on  traditional faxing?

Zoom struggles with traditional office needs like complex call  routing, multi-level menus, and reliable faxing.

By the time you add up phone licenses, meetings, and AI,  what is your actual per-user cost compared to the price  you saw on their website?

By the time you add ÔÇťunlimitedÔÇŁ calling and analytics, that  ÔÇťcheapÔÇŁ license often costs more than our all-in bundle.

Zoom

Your IT team will spend hours every week troubleshooting  ÔÇťversion mismatchÔÇŁ issues and mandatory security updates for  every employee.

How much time does your IT team spend troubleshooting  ÔÇśuser errorÔÇÖ or update issues when employees are just  trying to join a meeting?

THIRD-PARTY PROOF & RESOURCES

Uptime Guarantee

9th Consecutive JD Power Award

SecuriSync

Ranked as a ÔÇťLeaderÔÇŁ in the Frost & Sullivan Radar RELEVANT COLLATERAL UC Brochure - Abbreviated

UC Brochure - Flipbook

UC for Teams

UC Plan Comparison - Internal Only

Contact Center Data Sheet

OVERVIEW

WHAT ARE WE ANNOUCING?

How fast is 5G?

ÔÇó  Speeds can range from ~100Mbps to >200Mbps

ÔÇó  Compare ÔÇô LTE Average Range is 20-25Mbps

ÔÇó  Baseline 5G is 4 ÔÇô 5 times faster than LTE, and could be higherÔÇŽ

ÔÇó Verizon is rate limited to 100Mbps on our plan

ÔÇó AT&T tested at 239 Mbps down, 22Mbps up.

ÔÇó Remember ÔÇô Wireless is not like fiber ÔÇô not symmetrical and every location is different!

ÔÇó  Wireless bandwidth is always best effort -  speeds can vary by carrier, location, signal strength

and quality, proximity to cell tower and other environmental factors. The underlying wireless carrier

may temporarily slow data speeds if network is busy.

OVERVIEW

G P C  P R O D U C T  L A U N C H HOW DOES IT WORK?

Wireless Backup 5G - Automatic Failover

ÔÇó Cradlepoint Monitors flow of traffic to  the Internet

ÔÇó If an interruption of Internet traffic is  detected, router will automatically  switch over to wireless LTE.

ÔÇó Failover takes around one minute.

ÔÇó Router will automatically switch back  to landline when service is restored.

*Note:  Static IP addresses will work on the GPC landline Internet, but not during failover to the wireless network.

Confidential        ┬ę 2025 Great Plains Communications

ÔÇó No static IP on Wireless Internet  Broadband ÔÇô IP provided by wireless  carrier using DHCP, so servers hosted  on prem will not be reachable from the  Internet.

##### Body text (outside table regions)

GPC   CLOUD CONNECT

GPC Cloud Connect transforms the way businesses reach leading cloud services by providing  a fast, flexible and secure connection to Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, Google,  Oracle, IBM, SAP, Nutanix, Salesforce and Alibaba.

FEATURES & BENEFITS

ÔÇ║ Provides a fast, flexible and secure direct connection to multiple leading Cloud and  Network Providers on our Cloud ecosystem

ÔÇ║ Establishes connectivity without the need to own or manage a physical network

ÔÇ║ Secures traffic when connecting between metros and regions

ÔÇ║ Provides multi-region redundancy by connecting to multiple Cloud regions from a single  interconnection point

ÔÇ║ Provides direct Cloud connections with speeds up to 10 Gbps

CLOUD ECOSYSTEM

OMAHA

CHICAGO

Great Plains Communications TRANSPORT NETWORK

DENVER

Add GPC Cloud Connect to your Managed Ethernet network to begin accessing Cloud content  over your secure, private and direct connection. This direct conection provides reliable and  predictable network performance and increased efficiencies due to reduced latency.

GPC   DDOS PROTECTION

Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) Protection provides global, cloud-based traffic analysis and threat  detection combined with rapid attack mitigation. This multi-layered approach to DDoS protection is an  Enterprise best practice for mitigating todayÔÇÖs dynamic multi-vector DDoS attacks.

GPCÔÇÖs automated or on-demand attack traffic scrubbing service, backed by teams of network security  experts, defends your organization against high-volume DDoS attacks that threaten to block customers  from accessing websites and applications.

FEATURES

Proactive DDoS Protection

On-Demand DDoS Protection

ÔÇ║ Monitoring portal with network 
 traffic analysis

| BGP TRAFFIC                | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                             |

| **ASNs (All)**             | This report shows in and out data for a select customer, broken down by aggregate ASNs (origin and transit). Select which customerÔÇÖs data is displayed from the Customer drop-down menu.                |
| **ASNs (NULL)**            | This report shows in and out data for a selected customer for locally sourced or destined trafc based upon iBGP-only trafc. Select which customerÔÇÖs data is displayed from the Customer drop-down menu. |
| **ASNs (Origin)**          | This report shows in and out data for a selected customer, broken down by origin ASNs. Select which customerÔÇÖs data is displayed from the Customer drop-down menu.                                      |
| **ASNs (Peer)**            | This report shows in and out data for a selected customer, broken down by transit ASNs. Select which customerÔÇÖs data is displayed from the Customer drop-down menu.                                     |
| **NextHop**                | This report shows source and destination trafc data for a selected customer, broken down by NextHop. You can select which customerÔÇÖs data is displayed from the Customer drop-down menu.                |
| **GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION** | **DESCRIPTION**                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| **Cities**                 | This report shows trafc in and out of a selected customer broken down by the backbone city of origin.                                                                                                   |
| **Countries**              | This report shows trafc in and out of a selected customer broken down by the external geographic region of origin.                                                                                      |
| **Regions**                | This report shows trafc in and out of a selected customer broken down by the external geographic region of origin.                                                                                      |
| **IP INFORMATION**         | **DESCRIPTION**                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| **ICMP**                   | This report shows the trafc fowing into and out of a given customer, broken down by pairings of ICMP types and ICMP codes.                                                                              |
| **Packet Size**            | This report shows trafc fowing into and out of a customer, sorted by packet size.                                                                                                                       |
| **Protocols**              | This report shows trafc fowing into and out of a given customer, broken down by IP protocol.                                                                                                            |
| **TCP Applications**       | This report displays the trafc going in and out of a selected customer for the top TCP applications observed, broken down by application port.                                                          |
| **UDP Applications**       | This report displays the trafc going in and out of a selected customer for the top UDP applications observed, broken down by application port.                                                          |

| **IP Precedence**                                 | This report tracks the amount of trafc for each TOS precedence setting seen for the selected customer.
